name: Taralli Scaldati Col Finocchio (Taralli with Fennel Seeds
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the dried yeast into the warmed white wine and let proof for 10 minutes.
2. Add the olive oil, then combine with the flour, salt, fennel or black pepper.
3. Mix until the dough comes together, knead on a lightly floured board for 5 to 7 minutes.
4. Knead until the dough is smooth and responsive. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover and let rise until puffy but not doubled, about 1 to 1 1/4 hours.
5. Break off pieces of dough the size of a lime and roll between palms to form ropes about 18 inches long and as thin as breadsticks.
6. Let each one rest while you roll out the remaining (about 12 in total). Cut into lengths of 6 inches, connect their ends to form rings about 2 inches in diameter.
7. Press the ends together firmly; pinch tightly so the dough recovers.
8. Set the rings on oiled baking sheets, cover and let rise for 1 hour.
9. Preheat oven to 350°F. Bring a pot of water to boil.
10. When risen, plunge the taralli into the boiling water, a few at a time, until they float to the surface.
11. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
12. Let cool slightly, then bake on oiled baking sheets for about 20 to 24 minutes, until crunchy.
